message:
feat(studio): overhaul visualization engine and UI/UX

Major upgrade to the CMA-ES Studio visualization and interface:

Visualization Engine (Three.js):
- Implemented cinematic post-processing pipeline with UnrealBloomPass and ReinhardToneMapping for a 'neon-future' aesthetic.
- Replaced 2D confidence ring with a physically-based, semi-transparent 3D glass hyper-ellipsoid that morphs in real-time to visualize the covariance matrix.
- Added dynamic Major/Minor axes vectors to clearly show principal components.
- Optimized particle system using texture-based instanced rendering for high performance (60fps).
- Added glowing history trails to trace the optimization trajectory.
- Enhanced landscape rendering with vertex-colored Viridis heatmaps and physical materials.

UI/UX Modernization:
- Redesigned layout with a 'Stripe-like' premium aesthetic using Inter/JetBrains Mono typography and refined glassmorphism.
- Integrated Cupertino Pane for a native-feeling, physics-based mobile bottom sheet.
- Added comprehensive Haptic feedback (navigator.vibrate) for sliders and controls.
- Organized controls into a fixed desktop sidebar with collapsible 'Advanced Options'.
- Implemented robust Help and Tutorial overlays with keyboard shortcut guides.
- Improved responsiveness with floating HUD capsules and adaptive layouts.

Code Quality:
- Hardened error handling for optional DOM elements.
- Fixed memory leaks in Three.js resource management.
- Removed legacy code (Monaco editor, custom function buttons) to streamline the experience.
